reviews.review_text: game's fun but more importantly all the characters are hot

Love the game, but the battle pass needs to be amended to include player matches. I don't play against randos and only play this with my friends so it feels like a slap in the face that the cool stuff that I'd love to work towards is unavailable just because I don't play this competitively.
They added a fomo Battle Pass system for models and lore that should be in base game or DLC.

why are we putting a battle pass in a fighting game? genuinely i cannot see how this could benefit anyone. putting aside the cheap cost of said pass. why not just add it anyway? and why not make it something you can JUST buy? nothing has stopped them in the past when it comes to making cosmetics dlc. 25th anniversary taught us that. the game is fine gameplay wise, especially after 2.0 it can be fun, but by god do not promote this actual BAFFOONERY thats been caused by their greed. if a buddy or an influencer wants you to get this game and you REALLY want it. just pirate it. if any good changes are made regarding the battlepass, ill change my review.

TLDR: dont buy this game, dlc's, and especially the blazing pass. they have already siphoned so much money from us. dont let them take more.
Its a shame, watching what was one of the best fighting games of this generation, falling to the greed of battle passes like all the others have
Imagine adding fomo ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and a paid battle pass to a fighting game.
A "Blazing pass" really? the game is 5 years old already and you guys are adding a battle pass?

5 years into a game's lifespan and we're drastically changing the gameplay and adding battle passes... are we serious?

Because this worked soooo well for Tekken 8...

I have close to 1000 hours in this game between pc and console. I'm plat 3 and honestly, not bad. If you like fast paced, non stop action then you might consider strive. 

****IF YOU'RE A NEW PLAYER**** 
If this is your first fighting game, good luck. Don't give up. We need more players, please. 
The game is pretty simple to get into, but very complex once you understand it. The good thing for you is there's not an insane amount of clutter to learn at first. outside of knowing your moves, you have to keep track of at first is 

1; meter
2: burst meter

that's it.. unless you play Asuka. but don't do that to yourself. Or do, you masochist you. 

There's lots of characters, satisfying combos and everybody has their own fighting style and personality. they have easy characters, difficult and extremely hard characters. To put it into perspective how high the skill ceiling is, i've been playing a 4-star character(5-stars is the easiest to play) for 4 years and i still have a lot to learn.

i hope you give it a try, if you do feel free to add me and i'll show you guys the ropes. @Y.G.I.C (teyemfleyes)
Not a fan of the battle pass limited time stuff. I was happy that Strive didn't bother doing it when SF6 and Tekken did. Give us an option to buy the content after the pass is over normally.

devs wanted to attract new players, so they humiliated their old playerbase. well, they made their money - now its time to squeeze the remaining few players for a few more dollars with a battle pass every few months. after seeing certain infinites being introduced in this patch ii believe they stopped the little playtesting they did to have more time to develop more skins. 

Guilty Gear used to be fun because its so crazy - Strive prefers esport balance, so it moves closer to Street Fighter with every patch - more streamlined general mechanics, simplified, easier to balance characters, streamlined frames on buttons, character weights and combo routes. 

i think guilty gear strive is what happens, when developers start being ignorant about what made a character fun to begin with. Obviously doesn't apply to all characters..but i'd say quite a few.

Guess ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ is not blazing too much for me anymore.
Guilty Gear has been infected by time limited battle pass. FOMO is anti consumer practice that needs to be boycotted every time it is implemented.

i paid 60 dollars for this game at launch
bought over 60 more dollars worth of dlc for the next 4 years
then they add a battle pass randomly 5 years after launch????
as worthless as the battlepass is i will never recommend 120+ dollar game to any newcomers and this recent addition has fully cemented my opinion

price aside this game is amazing and if you happen to be rich then its really quite good
balance is really good no matter how many people try to complain about it
the cast is extremely diverse in gameplay, character, and design
game is a 9/10 ignoring price

mods still work btw

This will probably be my favorite fighting game forever.
I've tried Street Fighter and Smash but Guilty Gear is just too sick. I manages to be a good game AND a really unique one at the same time. You can't really get that with SF when everyone is spamming drive rush and uses low medium kick.
The mechanics are really cool, especially the big gimmick of roman cancels. Frame data is so important to fighting games, so why not spend some resources to just ignore it?
But enough glazing. The maps in this game are really inconsistent in performance. Ignore this if you have a relevant dedicated GPU. You can be perfectly running one  while another is unplayable. I know its better than nothing but someone might think they can run perfectly but once they try online their frames get halved and they're suddenly a potato playing loser.
This game is hard, too. Like all fighting games, the essence of playing has a higher skill ceiling compared to more popular genres. It takes like 20 hours for someone to realize that its just chess without turns. But if you're reading this review then I guess you're interested enough. If there were better in game resources to learn that would be great. Learning matchups is a chore, too. Some things are just not as obvious as they should be and can win matches easily. Having to look up frame data is kinda a must if you want to improve. And every character is just too strong to ignore with pure fundamentals unless your adaptability is better than mahoraga.
Idc about the battle pass tho. I'll get mad if they lock skins from older games behind FOMO.

Overall, greatly designed game for a fighter that feels really nice to play. Sick characters too. However, learning the game is pretty rough without youtube and stuff.
It's a great fighting game with even more amazing art and music. I, however, am absolutely garbage at it lol. Still a strong recommendation though if you enjoy fighting games.
Arcsys forbid technical characters be good, I hate it here.
It's a good game, but the devs decided to add limited time FOMO battlepass ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Plenty of other great fighting games that actually respect your time.

the game a huge cesspool of the most unwashed baboons I have ever had the misfortune of coming across in all my years in the fgc, the balancing is awful(as of the latest version which is 2.0), the roster is full of characters that promote the most degenerate and boring of playstyles, ultimately causing the overall experience of the game to be unenjoyable and just not fun.

So dear reader... if you're looking to buy this game and its mostly pay to win DLC... don't let this review stop you. because if you go through with this decision, you're probably too far gone, and need to get on indeed.com or better yet any creative software.

- This one made me get into fighting games more seriously. It's just so fun.
- Got in and bought Jam because I thought she was really cool and fun to play with. Worth it
- Story mode is a giant movie. I see
- Got to gold 1 in a short period of time. Let's see if I can get more eventually.
- Love the mechanics and characters. Music is awesome.

Decent fighting game. They gutted a ton of characters in the 2.0 update but I mean it was worth it. It is very beginner friendly and has a decently high skill ceiling. Compared to SF6 however, Strive is less about neutral and fundamentals and more about mixups and offense. I personally don't like hyper offensive games but I play Axl so I get to play neutral. Also happy chaos infinite.
Loads of fun. Was looking to get back into fighting games after a long break and I'm hooked.

Not a fan of the direction the game is going in. A full on crossover character was definitely a bad sign, but a battle pass and dailys are just not it for me. I hadn't regretted how much money I've spent on this game, buying every dlc pack despite only despite only really playing two characters that come with the base game, until today. I don't intend to make any additional purchases from this point onward.

A.B.A 2.0:
Jealousy Gauge: Initial value changed from 50% -> 25%.
Jealousy Gauge Depletion: A 10F recovery period now occurs after the superflash ends.
Install: Recovery amount of the Jealousy Gauge changed from 50% -> 25% of what was consumed.
Keygrab: 12F -> 14F, on block +2F -> -2F
2P: 4F->5F
Do you recommend this game?: Yes -> No
Good game but nontheless there's 17 7$ characters in a 40$ game but it does go on sale

I want to love this game so bad but i swear the devs have it out for me, i learned the game with ram who is a great character but didnt fit my play style but then i tried out testament and they fit me PERFECTLY but for soooome reason the devs nerf them every patch for no particular reason and right now is by far the worst time to play strive as the top tiers are barely playing the same game so for that reason i cant recommend it right now

Just don´t like the style, i liked the R+ style with blood, Horror and Monsters and faust being the funny goofy Doctor.
This is just not doing it for me.
Gameplay is fun but not enough, no real air combos except dust Combos.
Wen you have no Meter it stops being fun until you can rc again.
Sooo not a fan.
Just buy it. The sound track alone is peak. Each character feels so unique, and the techs in this game are cool.
Very stylish game! Kind of overwhelming to learn as a beginner, but cool enough to motivate me to learn anyways. Plus I've heard any fighting game has kind of a learning curve anyways!

Love this game so much its easy to get into and the characters are so good looking and fun. music is some of my favorite of all time and I would highly recommend anyone looking for a fighting game should try this game.

I like the characters design aesthetics and gameplay. Fun controls. Good OST. Runs perfect on Linux. 

Cons: No 21:9 support

Pros: Bridget <3
Really great game and probably the best platform fighter I have ever played; Amazing character design

the story, animations and music are orgasm inducing, no complaints.
though the actual gameplay is good but is better for newcomers since theres less for more experienced players who want to sink their teeth into a fighting game like xrd.
Never been a fighting game type of guy mostly just played a bit of mortal kombat from time to time but GGST  really opened my eyes and is in my opinion the king of fighting games for me atleast

Look up a beginner's guide and you too can go from having no idea what's happening to having an exhilarating time getting your butt kicked in online matches. Arcade mode is fun too. There are plenty of starting characters and alternate colors so buying DLC is very optional IMO. Shoutout to my boys Anji and Axl.

if you're new to fighters and wanna try em out, this is the one ngl. has enough sauce so the ceiling isn't low, has a diverse roster in terms of design and playstyle, but is approachable for beginners. also it doesn't resort to using simple controls as their control scheme, so you get your fingers adjusted to doing standard motions of fighters like quarter and half circles. overall a great experience.
Get stomped, stomp someone, repeat, but have fun nonethless. :3 (this is the low level bridget way)
I bought this game on a random whim because it was on sale. I have now played for three hundred hours and have no intent to stop. Yeah, good game.

Guilty gear strive is a faithful continuation to the Guilty Gear series. In terms of adapting and adding new characters they went above and beyond delivering an amazing fighting game that i can go back to numerous times throughout my life and will visit it again many more times. In addition, the music slaps super hard.

Runs perfect on Linux and Steamdeck. Linux users, this is the fighting game for you.

This is solid. From the first arcade fight right to the end and then online, and some of the other modes, pace is on track. Great sound, music, animation. Game feels snappy, hits feel nice, I lose but I want to keep going. 

I havent touched a fighting game since the old SF2 days. Played Skullgirls2 for a few hours, and got hungry for something way more. GG: Strive was the right game to pick. 

Getting smashed a lot online haha. I have rocked up to the party a bit late, so I have quite a wall in front of me. (13 hours at time of review, ill get better).

Support for the game is ongoing atm. There was word end of last month that they are thinking about the next GG, but still are actively supporting this one. Any type of "next GG" will still be a few years away. Players numbers seem to be okay at times, getting matches without much of a wait.

Characters are mostly unlocked via paid content, as these characters were released after the first game at different dates along with more story etc. So there is a bit of DLC, maybe all up the game will total $100. Try and get things on sale. You dont need to buy everything, or even all at once. Go for characters that you are interested in. This is mostly okay.

The battlepass however.... Ive seen a lot of bps and usually they are pretty good but this one is by far the worst bp I have ever seen in a game ever. I dont even think I will ever bother (unless theres something relating to character I play). Its like 5-7 bucks depending on where you live in the world. Generally bps are like 10-20. I dunno the price part isnt that bad, its just that its a rly bad pass lol. Might need to rethink that one chief. 

Anyway good fighter with a hell of an online learning curve, plenty to do in single player modes. Runs smooth. Still with active support and also cross play. Get it full price or on sale, you wont be disappointed.

Yes. Just yes. Best character designs in modern gaming.
Only gripe is the ridiculous amout of micro-transactions (stages, color pallets, characters that were free in previous titles). That alone brings this game down from a 9/10 to a 7/10 for me. But all & all I still reccomend.

And its also worth noting and the developers actively listen to community feedback.

Easy enough to pickup compared to its competitor modern fighters (SF6, Tekken, etc), whilst maintaining it's own depth via unique system mechanics like Roman Cancels. Solid tutorial.

Easy to run irrespective of hardware. One of the best soundtracks of any video game in recent memory.

Can be a little explosive in it's combat as is normal for anime fighters, but if you're fine with that, I'd highly recommend the game.

Trash game

No characters are fun to fight, too many Happy Chaos and Queen Dizzy and Unika players in ranked. Go play Tekken or an actual good fighting game that isn't this rubbish 

Only good things are the characters and the soundtrack, you'll have more fun LARPing the game like Resident Evil players than playing it bc it's so unenjoyable. People that defend it have sunk cost fallacy from sinking hours of their pathetic lives to be good at a game only 1k people play so they think they have to keep playing. Despicable piece of gaming content.

Everything about this game is terrible 0/10 until you see two characters on screen fighting each other (not including the UI on that battle screen).  I fear its too late.  Our wallets and time have already been swindled.

The battle pass is the final nail in the coffin.

Great art and music, hope they keep adding complexity to the game and bring it closer to the OG Guilty Gears.

Very fun movesets and the roman cancel system makes for fun entertaining and unique outcomes.

Arcsys managed to simplify fighting games while also letting them remain deep and giving you lots of freedom to express yourself. To learn fighting games, you have to understand the mechanics of the game and arcsys has a mission system to do that. With this, it is possibly the best game I know of to learn.

This game is awesome and beautiful! Let it be your introduction to fighting games if you have never learned any before.
Music is fire, visuals are best in class, gameplay is pretty good once you find a character you like
Best, most accessible fighting game I've ever played. So much fun, perfect mechanics, amazing characters and music. 10/10
Really fun game but it'll be even better without Ky Unika or Dizzy. (Sin's okay i like the cement boy)
Still learning but extremely fun to play with friends.

the game is fun BUT there are a lot of caveats

1- if your region isn't highly populated, you're going to struggle with finding games
2- if your opponent has a bad PC, your game will lag too (yes, for some reason your pc can't render the game unless your opponent's can)
3- you need to learn the moves of every character in order to play against them effectively, and if you don't have the DLC... tough luck

It's a fun and fast paced fighting game with fantastic animation.  The only issue I have with it is the story mode, but any fighting game isn't expected to have a fantastic story mode.  Also the lore is insane.

One of my favourite fighting games ever.
First of all it has some of the coolest character designs in a video game i've ever seen.
A witch dressed in leather who fights with her electric guitar?
A big dude swinging around a huge metal coffin that has an alien inside it?
A cute nun-girl on roller blades fighting with a yoyo?
The games oozes style and charisma.

The combos are very fun to execute, and are just long enough that they're satisfying to execute without being so long that it becomes annoying to be on the receiving end.
There is something just so juicy and cathartic about landing a counter hit in this game. The whole screen slows, 
 the announcer goes COUNTER and you see the word COUNTER in big ass letters in the background. The dopamine hit you get never gets old. 

Now if you want a slow paced, honest feeling fighting game that rewards patience, this game isn't for you. This is a game of ridiculously strong offence, suffocating zoners and insane mixups. It's a game of who is capable of enforcing the most obnoxious ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, and it embraces this identity. If you like a fast paced, ''♥♥♥♥ it we ball'', active type of game, you'll love it just as much as I do.

My favorite fighting game. The music, the animation, the characters and the game are so much fun. This was my first traditional fighting game I took seriously and I enjoyed every second of learning it. If you want a fighting game to learn or want to get into them, play this game
the current patch genuinely gives me copious amount of depression, still love this game tho despite only really hanging onto this game for Robo ky (coming soon :D).

Easily in the top 5 best fighting games right now. You need a mod to find a match much faster, the soundtrack is one of the best in a fighting game and characters are charismatic each in their own way. Plus a 2.0 version is on the way bringing a lot of new stuff and new mechanics.

I'm finding this to be a fun introduction to taking fighting games seriously. The ranking system works well with matching your skill level to your opponents, and so far it feels well balanced with system mechanics and player skill being more important than using meta characters. The combos and inputs aren't too difficult, and the character designs are awesome in terms of aesthetics and gameplay.

never felt the kind of hatred for any video game character the same way I felt toward Elphelt and Unika.
They should be publicly executed
I like this game it has good graphics and cool character designs.
It has a easy combat system to get into, yet it's still complex to entertain you

I'm so dissaponted by the 2.0 update. big promises but with in the end we got a game with a less balanced state, 4 character assassinations and much less sauce. One step forward, two steps backwards. I had so much fun before 2.0 even with all the nonsense the game had.

do not listen to NIL its a 10/10 fighting game
also I'm once again asking for I-No's dive to be +5 on hit
Crazy how, at the time of writing, you have to pay an extra 126 USD to play with half of the cast.

this game is trash if you like fighting games that make sense. the devs obviously were smoking meth when they designed the frame data and mechanics of half the roster. i got it on sale for 20 dollars and id spend an extra 20 dollars to wipe this experience from my memory.
don't even think about it if you haven't been stanning this game for years. reviews r positive but everyone is gooning characters in the comments.

uninstall hidden: one of those

Amazing fighting game, one of the only big fighting games currently that doesn't add some ridiculously dumbed down version of controls to over-cater to new players (sf6, tekken especially). Characters are all fun to play, visuals are fun, mechanics are solid. Update 2.0 is probably the best point so far the game has ever been for new players to get into

One of my favorite fighting games ever.

I absolutely LOVE the music. It’s totally my kind of style. It’s actually very rare for me to listen to a composer or soundtrack where I love every single song, but in this game I genuinely enjoy every track.

About the visuals — honestly, I preferred the art style in the previous game. I think the older title looked better graphically, and the outfits were more exposed and eye-candy compared to this one. Thankfully mods work, and I use Blender myself, so I can customize things the way I like.

As for the game and story, I love Guilty Gear because of its lore. I’ve listened many times to explanations about how the world was created, because I’m fascinated by the mix of real history and fantasy elements — combining facts from our world with fiction in such a unique way.

The story in this game is okay, although I don’t really like the main villain >_<

Arcade mode stories are fun too, and the mini stories are enjoyable. Lucy is honestly one of the best guest DLC characters I’ve ever seen. I only wish 2B was here too 

About online play — there are many weaker players like me, and I mostly play in Bronze ranks while having a great time. People randomly press buttons and attack blindly, while I enjoy spending hours practicing one thing and then testing myself online.

I also don’t stick to just one character because I like almost all of them, so I play a little bit of everyone.

I think this game is very good for new players. Combos feel much easier to execute here than in Street Fighter 6. The difficulty level in that aspect feels closer to Tekken 8.

And I LOVE Roman Cancel. It’s such a fun mechanic. I enjoy it much more than the systems in SF6.

I regularly play SF6, GBVS, and GGS <3

This fighting game  will make your ass burn. Heaven or Hell 
Daisuke Ishiwatari absolutely cooked.This game is literal cinema.I have 2000 hours here (Altogether on my second account too) .My social life is completely cooked.My grass-touching privileges are revoked.Do I regret it?No.The soundtrack goes insanely hard.It is a metal album.It just has a free fighting game attached.The rollback netcode is S tier. Zero lag against McDonalds Wi-Fi.Hitting a perfect Roman Cancel?Pure dopamine injection. Getting deleted by Potemkin?You just salute and accept it.It is not a skill issue.It is a lifestyle.Mankind blamed the beasts.I blame my sleep schedule.Buy it right now.Let them cook (you)).
Things go south for a long time, but now they decided to do limited battle passes, discouraging new players, since they will miss much content forever.

playing my Plat Pot is the greatest high on the planet

playing my Gold Zato is CBT
truly a community of some of the greatest most educated glue connoisseurs, also Millia pls stop mixing my ♥♥♥♥
its a great game its just a kind of dead game and the only people who you'll verse are people who're veterans at this game and refuse to let the game die with thousands of hours into the game

Daisuke and Naoki are still gods. I keep trying to love this game, but every time I come back to it, it ends up being another disappointment. All I wanted was for my favorite characters to come back, and somehow they actually did. I genuinely couldn’t believe it. If only the characters weren’t so gutted from the previous games :/ Fighting games need to stop throwing away what made them fun.

… That is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ blazing
Still my heart is blazing
If the words killed me
I don't need a new world order
You, soon you will know
We already know the smell of the-
Know the smell of the-
Know the smell of the-
Know the smell of the game

As a Bridget main, 2.00 is pretty trash. Let's just change and remove everything, huh? Guess i'll still play it though. The idea of roman canceling DP and long string BNB combos no longer exist, among other changes that followed. It gave the game an unique identity in the fighting game space which made it stand out from the rest. Now it's just a generic fighter that's been watered down. 

1/5. 2.00 was a mistake.

Pros: Probably the most fun I've had with any fighting game from this generation (Street Fighter 6, 2XKO, Tekken 8, etc.), unmatched style and visuals, ranked mode gets me matches in less than a couple minutes on average, really diverse roster in both visuals and playstyle, excellent balance compared to other games, and stellar dev support.

Cons: Some characters are just cancer to play against, online play outside of private matches and ranked is pretty bad most of the time, the tutorial barely teaches you and instead nudges you towards the actual learning mode in missions, story mode is literally just one long cutscene with no gameplay to speak of.

Only common complaint I don't feel like putting on the record is that the returning characters feel like simplified and slightly more boring versions of their previous versions. Even with that being somewhat true, you're still gonna have a lot to work with if you're coming in from games like Street Fighter where emphasis is more on system mechanics than individual character expression, and if this is your first fighting game, then you'll be even more shocked at what each character can do, and you'll probably be spoiled by this game considering the other most popular games are either never updated and stagnate a ton, are so horribly mismanaged that fans genuinely disowned them from their franchises, or is 2XKO.

Overall: 9/10. highly recommend for both FGC veterans and newcomers to the fighting game genre alike

Mankind knew that they cannot change society.
So instead of reflecting on themselves, they blamed the other player.
Battlepass + censorship compared to older games.

I am done with my recently rekindled cope about this game and series having a future at this point. This entire dev team needs to be fired and replaced at this point ESPECIALLY AND PARTICULARLLY any high level executive who signed off on the balancing and horrific grooming subplot that is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ small cell cancer to casual player appeal. The smash players and reddit groomers who will undoubtably clutch pearls and run defense for this ♥♥♥♥ can have the corpse of this franchise. Most normal members of the FGC have already moved on. Daisuke Ishiwatari has pissed away any respect I and many others had for him. This franchise is now on borrowed time, if it wasn't made clear enough by the complete, utter, and abject failure of Dual Rulers a year or so ago. And this isn't even covering them money grubbing their few remaining customers that still play this complete failure of a game that this ♥♥♥♥ is now in 2026. ♥♥♥♥ this company
game fun. beat ppl with yoyo's and ♥♥♥♥. oh yea. woohoo :)
Gay dudes that are hot fight each other. oh and women I guess
I'm not a fan of this new battle pass stuff, and I am not a fan of the balance on this patch.

If you're struggling then remember this sage advice: The fight begins on menu screen, not the battle itself. No, I'm not talking about character select, I'm talking about the amount of aura your player name and R-Code have. 

I ain't scared of a player named Slash, Jake or Hotashi; I'm afraid of the Back-throw Bandit and Brum Brum Unicar.
After 100h of training mode i finally can join online play and hate Happy Chaos there too
Battlepass gross and bad
Honestly brah, if you ignore the constant ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥’ on social media and instead focus on having fun with your friends in private rooms, you’re bound to have some fun!
I get to beat up people as a cute trans girl. Just like real life. Happy Pride!

Every network option is separated and behind a 3 seconsd "Connecting to the network" or "loading" screen.

It's a pain in the ass navigating to the network section you want.

Can't see my match history like other fighting games, the only option Is going to the theater, which is also behind these "Connecting" messages.

I don't know why this is not like street fighter where you connect once and every section is available to you without these 3s annoying messages.

In online, you can have up to 7 frames of delay depending on the opponent... SEVEN.

The mission modes are kinda annoyig, every try is behind a 3 second countdown, unlike trials mode in SF6 where you're there in the lab until you do what's specified in the mission.


There's a looooot of hoping through menus an other stuff that is annoying to get through. To cancel matchmaking you have to press a button 3 seconds, which is ok to avoid accidental cancelations.   BUT AFTER PRESSING IT FOR 3 SECONDS THE GAME STILL ASKS FOR YET ANOTHER CONFIRMATION.  Which justt ads to the pile of time wasting things this games  has that removes actual playing time


Artstyle, animations and character desing are world class tho, and this looks like I'm glazing SF6, but after having that I can't help but notice how bad this game on the QOL/technical side of things.
I'm not here to fit in this world.......I just want.......to make.................A difference. Maybe someday.......a dommy mommy Paracelsus.......could find me.
Everything about this series is insane in the best way possible
Pilebunker loops are the greatest thing humanity has ever made
Highly adictive and fun game fun
Remove Elphelt and this game is the best thing that has ever been made in the history of mankind
The game is very good. The menus and lobby system are dog.
you get ur ass whooped for like 20 hours and then you start understanding it
good fighting game, play asuka
fun w/ friends, without friends not as fun

The characters in this game are designed to perfection! Everyone giving a distinct visual style and gameplay unique to this game alone. This beautiful graphical art style is what ARCSYS is known for but in its best form here then their other games. The game has so many tools to introduce newer players to fighting games and get them to dig deeper with the mechanics. This game just has too many characters I love the aesthetics of. Asuka R, Venom, Axl, and Anji my beloveds!
something something cool game something something im bad something something get game something something
why is there a $4 battle pass for a game I spent $120 on. where am I.
Dang, you mean this really great soundtrack comes with a WHOLE GAME?!  And it's a fun one?!
Blazing Pass, Duel 1: Reignited, Launches!
